p.em: Intel Manual Quality Specification — Memory & State Transition

ul.li: addr=0, len=0: Application provides no pointer; kernel must provide the buffer.
buf_group (Offset 0x24): Points to the Ring Buffer Group ID registered via io_uring_register_buf_ring.
IOSQE_BUFFER_SELECT: Bit 9 in flags (or addr_flags in newer kernels) signals kernel-side allocation.

ol.li: Atomic read of ring head index.
Increment Ring Head: Buffer is now "leased" to the kernel.

ul.li: Kernel fills bid=0 at physical address 0x7f00.
Payload size (1024) is recorded for the res field.
Buffer is marked as Reserved (cannot be selected for other SQEs).

text.tspan: // bid is encoded in top 16 bits of flags
// bid is encoded in top 16 bits of flags

div.p: Ownership Transfer: The kernel has released the buffer to the application. The application is responsible for the buffer until it is explicitly returned to the ring for reuse.

ol.li: App adds buffer back to the ring at the current tail.
Buffer is once again eligible for kernel selection.
